title: The Narrow Way
subtitle: The Lord seals His sermon on the mountain: strive to enter by the strait gate

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the broad road fall away.
Few there are who find the narrow path; sit quietly and seek it.
Let your heart be drawn upward, away from the comfort of the body.
gospel: Enter ye in at the strait gate:
for wide is the gate, and broad is the way that leadeth to destruction,
and many there are who go in thereat.
How strait is the gate, and narrow is the way that leadeth unto life!
And few there are that find it.
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold the Lord, who had commanded many and wondrous things,
and had ordered His own to be free from all sufferings.
Hear Him add this warning, lest anyone say these things are unbearable, hard, the way too strait.
Behold Him say: Strive, then, and labor anxiously to enter by the narrow gate, not the wide one.
As if He said: Though these things be hard and narrow, yet they merit life and are the entrance to heaven.
And mark well that He says Strive — for the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent bear it away.
For it cannot be done without great striving, that an earthly man become a citizen in heaven.
consider: To fast, to keep vigil, to abstain from the flesh's desires, to do not one's own will — to whom does this not seem strait and narrow?
But to eat and drink with delight, to obey every craving of the flesh, to contradict one's will in nothing — to whom does this not seem wide and spacious?
But, alas! by the broad way almost all of us walk, and for the narrow few of us care.
We who were commanded to walk by the narrow gate walk ever by the wide.
That men of the world should tread the broad and spacious way is no great wonder;
but that those who seem to have taken up their cross and to follow Christ should refuse the narrow — let it not seem a marvel to you, let it seem a portent!
Look: he who serves the princes of this world refuses no labor, shuns no danger, makes excuse for no indignity — all in hope of gain.
He fears not death in foreign lands, bears the absence of wife and children, is moved by no homeland, no affection for his own; burning with love of money, he feels no toil, knows no grief.
And we, who seek not money but wisdom, who ask not earth but heaven — we inquire about the body's rest!
See how much more wretched than they we are, and how much softer.

What do you say, O man, what do you do? You make ready to climb to heaven, and you ask whether some difficulty may meet you on the road — and you do not blush, but, crushed by no shame, you bury yourself beneath the earth!

If all evils should rush upon you — sword, iron, fire, beast, famine, sickness, every evil that can be spoken or thought — it would be fitting that none of these should seem to you anything but laughable, and utterly to be despised, for so great and such a cause.
For this is the fear of an old woman's soul, and an effeminate one.
The narrow way is no broad road but a footpath, on which each is anxiously bound who keeps himself watchfully hemmed in under the guard of the commandments.

Is it not a kind of narrowness of the road — to live in this world, yet to have nothing of this world's desire; to crave not what is another's, to hold not even one's own; to despise the praise of the world, and for God's sake to love reproach; to flee glory and follow contempt?
These are footpaths, but great ones: for as much as in this life they are narrow for the keeping of right living, so much the more in the eternal reward are they widened.
prayer: make me, by the narrow way of justice and the strait gate of penance, to enter into the hall of salvation and the life of glory.
Teach me to shun the deceit of the deceivers,
and grant me to imitate the simplicity and innocence of the spiritual sheep;
make me fix the root of my heart in heaven, not on earth,
that I may deserve to be found faithful not in the leaves of words only, but rather in the fruits of good works.
Make me do the will of the heavenly Father,
and, hearing Thy words, fulfill them in deed,
that, made firm in Thee, I may be parted from Thee by no temptations. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quam angusta porta et arcta via est, quæ ducit ad vitam; et pauci sunt qui inveniunt eam!
latinJewel.english: How strait is the gate, and narrow is the way that leadeth unto life; and few there are that find it!
